# Telegram Channel

## Overview

Telegram bot channel — long-poll worker delivering MCP-driven conversations to a per-org BotFather bot.

The Telegram worker runs in the ``telegram_worker`` container. It polls every active ``GSageInterfaceProfile`` with ``interface = 'telegram'`` and starts a long-poll loop for each bot using the token stored in ``interface_config.bot_token``. New bots are picked up after at most ``TELEGRAM_RELOAD_INTERVAL`` seconds.

## Quick facts

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Interface key | `telegram` |
| Config storage | `interface_profile.interface_config` |
| CLI module | `src.ops_cli.channels.telegram` |
| Worker modules | `src.telegram_worker.main` |

## Per-organisation configuration

> The JSON below describes the per-organisation configuration. It is stored in `GSageInterfaceProfile.interface_config` (JSONB column) and managed via `python -m ops_cli channels telegram …`.

> **Sensitive fields** (passwords, tokens, secrets) are currently stored *as-is* in the JSONB column — gSage does not yet encrypt them at rest. Treat the database as a secret store and provide values through the CLI's `--*-stdin` flags so they never appear in argv or shell history.

### Empty template (required fields only)

```json
{
  "bot_token": "<value>"
}
```

### Example payload

```json
{
  "bot_token": "<value>"
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Required | Sensitive | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | :---: | :---: | --- | --- |
| `bot_token` | `string` | ✓ | ✓ | — | BotFather token (e.g. ``123456789:ABC-DEF…``). |


## Environment variables

These cluster-wide variables seed the channel's runtime defaults. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) before starting the worker. **Per-org configuration** (see the previous section) takes precedence at runtime.

| Variable | Type | Default | Sensitive | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| `TELEGRAM_MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH` | `integer` | `4096` | — | Outbound chunk size before splitting (Telegram hard cap is 4096 characters). |
| `TELEGRAM_RATE_LIMIT_ORG_DAILY` | `integer` | `200` | — | Maximum messages forwarded per organisation per UTC day. |
| `TELEGRAM_RATE_LIMIT_USER_HOURLY` | `integer` | `30` | — | Maximum messages forwarded per Telegram user per rolling hour. |
| `TELEGRAM_RELOAD_INTERVAL` | `integer` | `300` | — | Interval (s) between DB scans to discover new / deactivated bot profiles. ``0`` disables hot-reload. |

## CLI usage

**Create / update the org-wide Telegram profile**

```bash
echo '<bot-token>' | python -m ops_cli channels telegram upsert \
    --org-slug gsage \
    --description "Main SOC bot" \
    --bot-token-stdin
```

**Inspect the current profile (token redacted)**

```bash
python -m ops_cli channels telegram show --org-slug gsage
```


## Prerequisites

- A Telegram bot token issued by `@BotFather`.
- Network egress from the `telegram_worker` container to `api.telegram.org` (HTTPS).
- The `telegram_worker` service must be running (`docker compose up telegram_worker`).
